content.full_text:
FPPC

Secretary of State 3 49 09 IN 92

1230 J STREET
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814

September 1, 1992

Ms. Maryann Kvasager
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Maryann:

Pursuant to our telephone conversations, I am writing to
seek informal advice on behalf of Secretary of State March
Fong Eu and her former political committee regarding the
proper method of reporting receipts associated with
committee-sponsored trips. I have received oral advice from
commission staff over the years regarding such reporting
procedures but I have been unable to locate written responses
to inquiries. This current inquiry arises in the context of
the routine audit of political committees by the Franchise Tax
Board.

In 1988, the Friends of March Fong Eu (now terminated)
sponsored a trip to promote California trade and investment
and to raise campaign funds. The campaign contracted with a
travel agency in order to handle the arrangements for the
trip. Each traveler paid the travel agency $3,300 for the
travel package. The actual cost of providing the trip by the
travel agency was $2,250. The fair market value of the trip
was deemed to be $2,500. The travel agency donated to the
campaign $1,050 per traveler. Of this amount, the campaign
reported $800 as being a contribution from the traveler and
$250 as being a contribution from the travel agency. This
method of reporting was consistent with the oral advice
received from commission staff and with the definition of a
"contribution" as being a payment "except to the extent that
full and adequate consideration is received".

I should very much appreciate receiving your informal
advice as to whether the committee properly reported these
transactions under the circumstances set forth.

Sincerely,

ANTHONY L. MILLER

cc: Linda Halverson,
Auditor, Franchise Tax Board

NOT PRINTED AT PUBLIC EXPENSE [seal] 16

sections.question: Whether the committee properly reported receipt transactions from a 1988 committee-sponsored trip to promote California trade and investment and raise campaign funds, where travelers paid $3,300 to a travel agency, the actual cost was $2,250, fair market value was $2,500, and the travel agency rebated $1,050 per traveler (reported as $800 contribution from traveler and $250 from travel agency).
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor seeks informal advice on behalf of Secretary of State March Fong Eu's former political committee regarding whether receipts from a committee-sponsored 1988 trip were properly reported, where the campaign received rebates from a travel agency that were allocated between traveler and travel agency contributions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter TO the FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. No conclusion/advice section is present in this document. The letter requests informal advice on contribution reporting from committee-sponsored trip transactions. Minor OCR issues present (e.g., 'aalvice' for 'advice', 'commi t tee' for 'committee').

embedding.summary: Anthony L. Miller requests informal FPPC advice on behalf of Secretary of State March Fong Eu's terminated political committee regarding the proper reporting method for receipts from a 1988 committee-sponsored trip, where travelers paid $3,300 but the travel agency rebated $1,050 per traveler that was reported as contributions.
